## Origins

Lord [Robert Reyne](wiki:Robert_Reyne), the Lord of [Castamere](wiki:Castamere), was succesful in arranging a betrothal between his daughter [Ellyn](wiki:Ellyn_Reyne) and [Tywald Lannister](wiki:Tywald_Lannister), the heir of Lord [Gerold Lannister](wiki:Gerold_Lannister) of [Casterly Rock](wiki:Casterly_Rock). However, both Ellyn's betrothed and father were slain during the [Peake Uprising](wiki:Peake_Uprising) in [233 AC](wiki:233_AC). Ellyn, strong-willed and hot-tempered, was not willing to give up on her dream of becoming Lady of Casterly Rock, something which she had been anticipating for years. She convinced Lord Gerold's second son, [Tion](wiki:Tion_Lannister), to break his own betrothal and marry her instead. In this she was succesfull; Ellyn and Tion were wed in [235 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), causing Ellyn to become the Lady of Casterly Rock in all but name, as Lord Gerold was widowed.(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_westerlands:_house_lannister_under_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

Ellyn used her position to support the position of [her own family](wiki:House_Reyne). In [236 AC](wiki:236_AC), she became a widow when Tion died in the [Fourth Blackfyre Rebellion](wiki:Fourth_Blackfyre_Rebellion). Ellyn's influence dwindled as Lord Gerold assumed more control over Casterly Rock and began to prepare his younger son Tytos for his future lordship. According to a semi-canon source, Ellyn at first claimed to have been pregnant shortly after Tion's death, but as time passed and her belly remained flat it was concluded the pregnancy had been a lie.(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-sample_2-2) Thereafter, most of the Reynes departed Casterly Rock for Castamere. Due to the [Widow's Law](wiki:Widow's_Law), Ellyn remained at the Rock, but her influence was declining sharply. In [239 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), Ellyn made another claim to the position she so desired and attempted to bed Tytos, Lord Gerold's new heir. The young Tytos was so intimidated by Ellyn that he was unable to perform and confessed what had happened to his wife, Lady [Jeyne Marbrand](wiki:Jeyne_Marbrand), who related the tale to Lord Gerold. Gerold acted at once, and Ellyn was hastily married to the twice-widowed [Walderan Tarbeck](wiki:Walderan_Tarbeck), Lord of [Tarbeck Hall](wiki:Tarbeck_Hall).(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_westerlands:_house_lannister_under_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4) The [rivalry](wiki:War_of_the_Wombs) between Ellyn and Jeyne, which had begun years past, would continue for many years to come.

[Tytos](wiki:Tytos_Lannister) became the [Lord of Casterly Rock](wiki:Lord_of_Casterly_Rock) and [Warden of the West](wiki:Warden_of_the_West) in [244 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest) at the death of Lord Gerold. Tytos desired to be loved, and as such was willing to forgive people quickly. Many saw this as a weakness, and as such, many of Tytos's bannermen and merchants from [Lannisport](wiki:Lannisport) and [Kayce](wiki:Kayce) borrowed money from him without paying their debts. Through her brothers, Lady Ellyn borrowed gold from [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) as well, which she used to restore the crumbling [Tarbeck Hall](wiki:Tarbeck_Hall). The power of House Lannister grew less, and even beyond the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) people began to realize the Lannisters were no longer to be feared.(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_westerlands:_house_lannister_under_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5)

Lord Tytos's eldest three sons left to fight in the [War of the Ninepenny Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Ninepenny_Kings) in [260 AC](wiki:260_AC). When [Tywin](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), Tytos's heir, returned from the [Stepstones](wiki:Stepstones) with his brothers, [Kevan](wiki:Kevan_Lannister) and [Tygett](wiki:Tygett_Lannister), he took upon himself to restore the power of House Lannister, despite Tytos's reluctance. Tywin demanded repayment of his father's loans and all who could not pay were ordered to send hostages to Casterly Rock. Kevan formed a new company of five hundred veterans to aid Tywin. Lord [Roger Reyne](wiki:Roger_Reyne), Ellyn's older brother, laughed when he read Tywin's edicts and advised his friends and vassals to do nothing. Lord Walderan Tarbeck hoped to convince Tytos to rescind Tywin's edicts and traveled to Casterly Rock, but was instead imprisoned by Tywin.(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_westerlands:_house_lannister_under_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6) In return, his wife Ellyn seized three Lannisters – two [Lannisters](wiki:House_Lannister_of_Lannisport) of [Lannisport](wiki:Lannisport), and [Stafford Lannister](wiki:Stafford_Lannister), whose sister [Joanna](wiki:Joanna_Lannister) was betrothed to Tywin – and threatened them harm unless her husband was returned. Tytos ignored Tywin's suggestion of sending Lord Walderan back to his wife in three pieces, one for every Lannister taken, and instead returned Walderan unharmed. In addition, he forgave the Tarbeck debt to House Lannister.(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-Raffc44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_westerlands:_house_lannister_under_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)

## Aftermath

By the end of the campaign the rebellious Houses [Reyne](wiki:House_Reyne) and [Tarbeck](wiki:House_Tarbeck) were completely obliterated. The ruined, blackened, and crumbling castles of [Castamere](wiki:Castamere) and [Tarbeck Hall](wiki:Tarbeck_Hall) stand empty to this day as a reminder to those who dare scorn the power of the Rock.(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-Rasos19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-Raffc33.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) Castamere's mines have remained sealed.(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_westerlands:_house_lannister_under_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-16)

Tywin's manner of dealing with the revolt had made him respected and feared throughout the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms).(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_westerlands:_house_lannister_under_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-17) Though the brutality of Tywin's methods drew censure from some, everyone agreed that order had been restored to the westerlands.(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_targaryen_kings:_aerys_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)

Tywin's actions to restore [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister)'s power are immortalized in the song "[The Rains of Castamere](wiki:The_Rains_of_Castamere)", and he is known to have used the song as a threat against troublesome bannermen.(wiki:Reyne-Tarbeck_revolt.cite_ref-Rasos19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-1)

## Origins

### Prelude[ ]

Seven-year-old Prince [Viserys Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_II_Targaryen) was taken captive at the end of [129 AC](wiki:129_AC) (shortly before the [Battle of the Gullet](wiki:Battle_of_the_Gullet) the following year) and then held captive in [Lys](wiki:Lys) during the [Dance of the Dragons](wiki:Dance_of_the_Dragons).(wiki:Lysene_Spring.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_the_voyage_of_alyn_oakenfest.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)
[House Rogare](wiki:House_Rogare) rose to prominence in [Lys](wiki:Lys) during the [Daughters' War](wiki:Daughters'_War), and their patriarch, [Lysandro](wiki:Lysandro_Rogare), wed Viserys to his youngest daughter, [Larra of Lys](wiki:Larra_Rogare). In [134 AC](wiki:134_AC) after meeting with Lysandro's brother, [Drazenko](wiki:Drazenko_Rogare), in [Sunspear](wiki:Sunspear), Lord [Alyn Velaryon](wiki:Alyn_Velaryon) traveled to Lys to negotiate with Lysandro a ransom for Viserys's return to the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms). Alyn agreed that the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) would pay one hundred thousand [golden dragons](wiki:Currency), utilize the [Rogare Bank](wiki:Rogare_Bank), uphold Viserys's marriage with Larra, and grant [lordships](wiki:Lord) to Lysandro's youngest sons.(wiki:Lysene_Spring.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_the_voyage_of_alyn_oakenfest.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)

### Prosperity[ ]

The return of Prince [Viserys Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_II_Targaryen) to [Westeros](wiki:Westeros) in [134 AC](wiki:134_AC) delighted his elder brother, King [Aegon III Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_III_Targaryen), who had felt guilty since the [Battle of the Gullet](wiki:Battle_of_the_Gullet), and caused the court at [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) to rejoice. The size of the ransom outraged Lord [Unwin Peake](wiki:Unwin_Peake), the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), however. Despite Unwin's objections, [Aegon's regents](wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III) agreed to pay the ransom in ten annual payments. They also agreed to transfer funds from the [Iron Bank of Braavos](wiki:Iron_Bank_of_Braavos) to the [Rogare Bank](wiki:Rogare_Bank) of [Lysandro](wiki:Lysandro_Rogare). Lord Peake resigned as Hand and was replaced with Lord [Thaddeus Rowan](wiki:Thaddeus_Rowan).(wiki:Lysene_Spring.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_the_voyage_of_alyn_oakenfest.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)
Some of [Larra Rogare](wiki:Larra_Rogare)'s brothers accompanied her to King's Landing, with [Moredo](wiki:Moredo_Rogare) leading her guards, [Lotho](wiki:Lotho_Rogare) opening a branch of the bank on [Visenya's Hill](wiki:Visenya's_Hill), and [Roggerio](wiki:Roggerio_Rogare) establishing the [Mermaid](wiki:Mermaid_(pillow_house)), a [pillow house](wiki:Pillow_house) at the [River Gate](wiki:River_Gate). Her uncle [Drazenko](wiki:Drazenko_Rogare) married [Aliandra Martell](wiki:Aliandra_Martell), [Princess of Dorne](wiki:Princess_of_Dorne).(wiki:Lysene_Spring.cite_ref-Rfabthe_lysene_spring_and_the_end_of_regency.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)
King's Landing prospered due to the Rogare Bank and increased trade with the [Free Cities](wiki:Free_Cities) of [Braavos](wiki:Braavos), [Lys](wiki:Lys), [Myr](wiki:Myr), [Pentos](wiki:Pentos), and [Tyrosh](wiki:Tyrosh). Other cities and towns of the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms) benefited as well, including [Duskendale](wiki:Duskendale), [Gulltown](wiki:Gulltown), [Hull](wiki:Hull), [Lannisport](wiki:Lannisport), [Maidenpool](wiki:Maidenpool), [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown), and [White Harbor](wiki:White_Harbor).(wiki:Lysene_Spring.cite_ref-Rfabthe_lysene_spring_and_the_end_of_regency.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)
For a time the Rogare Bank was stronger than even the Iron Bank of Braavos. The Rogare family's power helped to decrease the power of [Aegon's regents](wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III).(wiki:Lysene_Spring.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1) The Seven Kingdoms rejoiced first at the sight of Lady [Rhaena Targaryen](wiki:Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon)) flying atop [Morning](wiki:Morning) and then the birth of Prince [Aegon Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen), the son of Viserys and Larra.(wiki:Lysene_Spring.cite_ref-Rfabthe_lysene_spring_and_the_end_of_regency.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)

### Downfall[ ]

The [Lyseni](wiki:Lys) were distrusted by some in [Westeros](wiki:Westeros) due to their foreign customs and belief in Lysene gods instead of [the Seven](wiki:Faith_of_the_Seven). [Larra Rogare](wiki:Larra_Rogare)'s guards and companions were all Lyseni. King [Aegon III Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_III_Targaryen) and his [Hand](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), Lord [Thaddeus Rowan](wiki:Thaddeus_Rowan), were seen as ineffective at dealing with famine in the [north](wiki:North) and fighting in the [Vale of Arryn](wiki:Vale_of_Arryn) and the [Iron Islands](wiki:Iron_Islands). The hatching of a monster from [Laena Velaryon](wiki:Laena_Velaryon_(daughter_of_Alyn))'s [dragon egg](wiki:Dragon_egg) was seen as a bad omen, as was the [poisoning](wiki:Poison) of Aegon's friend, [Gaemon Palehair](wiki:Gaemon_Palehair).(wiki:Lysene_Spring.cite_ref-Rfabthe_lysene_spring_and_the_end_of_regency.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5)
[Lysandro](wiki:Lysandro_Rogare) and [Drazenko Rogare](wiki:Drazenko_Rogare) died a day apart from each other, with Drazenko choking on bacon and Lysandro drowning when his barge sank. Some attributed their abrupt deaths to the [Faceless Men](wiki:Faceless_Men) of [Braavos](wiki:Braavos). The deaths of the brothers led to a series of assassinations by the various [magisters](wiki:Magisters) and merchant princes of [Lys](wiki:Lys). Lysandro's eldest son, [Lysaro Rogare](wiki:Lysaro_Rogare), was an ineffective successor, and depositors began withdrawing their funds after rumors spread that the Rogare Bank was unsound. Lysaro fled Lys, but, after being caught in [Volon Therys](wiki:Volon_Therys), he was returned to Lys and scourged to death. [Drako](wiki:Drako_Rogare) reached [Volantis](wiki:Volantis) and [Marra](wiki:Marra_Rogare) claimed sanctuary in Lys's temple of [Yndros](wiki:Yndros), but their other kin lost their wealth or were sold into [slavery](wiki:Slavery).(wiki:Lysene_Spring.cite_ref-Rfabthe_lysene_spring_and_the_end_of_regency.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6)
In Westeros, Moredo surrendered his leadership of the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne)'s army in the [Vale of Arryn](wiki:Vale_of_Arryn) and sailed for Braavos. Lotho and Roggerio were imprisoned in the [Tower of the Hand](wiki:Tower_of_the_Hand) by Ser [Lucas Leygood](wiki:Lucas_Leygood) and Ser [Marston Waters](wiki:Marston_Waters), respectively, and the bank's branch on Visenya's Hill was seized. Lord Rowan was arrested, as were one of his nephews and twoscore grooms, servants, and knights retainer. King Aegon and Prince Viserys refused to allow Ser [Amaury Peake](wiki:Amaury_Peake) from entering [Maegor's Holdfast](wiki:Maegor's_Holdfast) and seizing Viserys's wife Larra, however. [Sandoq the Shadow](wiki:Sandoq_the_Shadow) slew Amaury and a dozen guards, allowing the royal brothers to seek refuge in Maegor's Holdfast for eighteen days.(wiki:Lysene_Spring.cite_ref-Rfabthe_lysene_spring_and_the_end_of_regency.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)
Ser Marston, the new Hand, refused to storm the keep during the so-called [secret siege](wiki:Secret_siege). Among other confessions, Thaddeus admitted to having helped loot the Rogare Bank and thereby defrauding Westerosi noblemen, as well as conspiring with Larra and her brothers to poison Aegon with the [Tears of Lys](wiki:Tears_of_Lys) in order to place Viserys on the Iron Throne. Aegon and Viserys determined that Lord Rowan was an innocent man tortured into making false confessions, however.(wiki:Lysene_Spring.cite_ref-Rfabthe_lysene_spring_and_the_end_of_regency.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7

## Origins

[Daenerys Targaryen](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) is [wed](wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen) to [Drogo](wiki:Drogo) as part of her brother [Viserys's](wiki:Viserys_Targaryen) alliance with the [khal](wiki:Khal).(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0) Drogo later kills Viserys at [Vaes Dothrak](wiki:Vaes_Dothrak),(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot46.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) however, and Drogo himself dies from wounds suffered in the [Dothraki sea](wiki:Dothraki_sea).(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) Daenerys hatches three [dragons](wiki:Dragon) from [dragon eggs](wiki:Dragon_egg) place in Drogo's funeral pyre,(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot72.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) and she eventually embarks on [a campaign](wiki:Targaryen_campaign_in_Slaver's_Bay) to end [slavery](wiki:Slavery) in [Slaver's Bay](wiki:Slaver's_Bay).(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Rasos27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Rasos42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Rasos57.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)
Daenerys chooses to rule as [Queen of Meereen](wiki:Queen_of_Meereen) after [conquering the city](wiki:Siege_of_Meereen),(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Rasos71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) and the widow refuses marriage offers from [Cleon the Great](wiki:Cleon_the_Great) of [Astapor](wiki:Astapor)(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Rasos71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1) and from [Xaro Xhoan Daxos](wiki:Xaro_Xhoan_Daxos) of [Qarth](wiki:Qarth).(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) Meanwhile, the [Sons of the Harpy](wiki:Sons_of_the_Harpy) resist Daenerys's rule over Meereen by engaging in a [shadow war](wiki:Shadow_war) against her supporters.(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) To bring peace to the city, [Galazza Galare](wiki:Galazza_Galare) advises Daenerys to marry a highborn Meereenese man of noble blood, suggesting [Hizdahr zo Loraq](wiki:Hizdahr_zo_Loraq). Daenerys tells Hizdahr she might be willing to marry him if he can prevent any murders in the city for ninety days and nights.(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)
Ser [Barristan Selmy](wiki:Barristan_Selmy), [Lord Commander of the Queensguard](wiki:Lord_Commander_of_the_Queensguard), is wary of the idea, telling Daenerys that the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms) of [Westeros](wiki:Westeros) would never accept Hizdahr as king.(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) [Daario Naharis](wiki:Daario_Naharis) suggests himself as a groom instead but is refused. After the [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) suggests that Daenerys massacre the [Great Masters](wiki:Great_Masters) at the wedding if she marries Hizdahr, the queen decides to send Daario back into the field.(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1)
[Reznak mo Reznak](wiki:Reznak_mo_Reznak) and Galazza, the [Green Grace](wiki:Green_Grace), discuss wedding preparations and customs with Daenerys. The queen rejects the custom of the bride being examined by the husband's female family members to test for fertility, as well as the custom of the bride washing the groom's feet. Daenerys would prefer to marry using Westerosi rites, but Galazza explains that would be unacceptable to the [Ghiscari](wiki:Ghiscari). Daenerys learns that the bride is expected to wear dark red veils above a [tokar](wiki:Tokar) of white [silk](wiki:Silk) fringed with baby [pearls](wiki:Pearl).(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-1) Hizdahr affirms for Daenerys that her enemies, including [Yunkai](wiki:Yunkai) and [New Ghis](wiki:New_Ghis), require her to wed as part of any peace treaty to end the [second siege of Meereen](wiki:Second_siege_of_Meereen).(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-2)
Daenerys sleeps with Daario when he returns to Meereen,(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-3) but continues to refuse his own marriage offers.(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) Prince [Quentyn Martell](wiki:Quentyn_Martell) arrives in Meereen to offer [his own hand in marriage](wiki:Secret_marriage_pact) as well, but he is refused by Daenerys as well.(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1) The queen commits instead to marrying Hizdahr the next day, though she sleeps with Daario one last time that night.(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-2)

## Aftermath

King Hizdahr decides to reopen the [fighting pits of Meereen](wiki:Fighting_pits_of_Meereen) to celebrate the city's peace with Yunkai, and he hosts a feast in the Great Pyramid for the Yunkishmen. Daenerys is displeased that a slave market has been opened by the [Wise Masters](wiki:Wise_Masters) south of the [Skahazadhan](wiki:Skahazadhan). Daario is angered by the marriage, so for his own protection Daenerys includes the sellsword among the hostages sent over the Yunkai'i.(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd50.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)

[Victarion Greyjoy](wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy) sails with the [Iron Fleet](wiki:Iron_Fleet) toward Meereen, having been sent by his brother [Euron](wiki:Euron_Greyjoy) to bring Daenerys to Westeros as the Crow's Eye's bride. Victarion intends to marry Daenerys himself, however, and after learning of her marriage he finds himself willing to kill Hizdahr to make Daenerys a widow again.(wiki:Wedding_of_Hizdahr_zo_Loraq_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0)

## Origins

Following the death of her husband, [Khal](wiki:Khal) [Drogo](wiki:Drogo), [Daenerys Targaryen](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) manages to hatch her three [dragon](wiki:Dragon) [eggs](wiki:Dragon_egg) on his funeral pyre.(wiki:Targaryen_campaign_in_Slaver's_Bay.cite_ref-Ragot72.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) As Drogo's [khalasar](wiki:Khalasar) has disbanded into several smaller factions, Daenerys sees herself forced to travel through the [Red Waste](wiki:Red_Waste), as she considers the chance of encountering an enemy too large when traveling in any other direction.(wiki:Targaryen_campaign_in_Slaver's_Bay.cite_ref-Racok12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) Daenerys eventually arrives in [Qarth](wiki:Qarth),(wiki:Targaryen_campaign_in_Slaver's_Bay.cite_ref-Racok27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) but following the destruction of the [House of the Undying](wiki:House_of_the_Undying) and the resulting hostility of the Qartheen, finds herself in need of a new destination.(wiki:Targaryen_campaign_in_Slaver's_Bay.cite_ref-Racok63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) Whilst looking for a passage on a ship at the docks of Qarth, Daenerys is approached by the eunuch [Belwas](wiki:Belwas) and the squire [Arstan Whitebeard](wiki:Barristan_Selmy) (secretly Ser [Barristan Selmy](wiki:Barristan_Selmy) from [Westeros](wiki:Westeros)), who were sent to [Qarth](wiki:Qarth) by [magister](wiki:Magister) [Illyrio Mopatis](wiki:Illyrio_Mopatis). Illyrio wishes her to return to [Pentos](wiki:Pentos), and has sent three ships to Qarth for Daenerys to use.(wiki:Targaryen_campaign_in_Slaver's_Bay.cite_ref-Racok63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1)

However, whilst on route to [Pentos](wiki:Pentos) with her dragons and the remnants of her khalasar, [Jorah Mormont](wiki:Jorah_Mormont) convinces Daenerys to change course to [Astapor](wiki:Astapor) instead, arguing that she will need an army to conquer Westeros. Jorah believes she should buy [Unsullied](wiki:Unsullied), slave soldiers trained and sold in [Astapor](wiki:Astapor) with an extraordinary reputation.(wiki:Targaryen_campaign_in_Slaver's_Bay.cite_ref-Rasos8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) In Astapor, Daenerys inspects the Unsullied, but is sickened by the misery of the slave trade.(wiki:Targaryen_campaign_in_Slaver's_Bay.cite_ref-Rasos23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) However, she agrees to acquire all the Unsullied available - eight thousand six hundred - as well as the boys still in training - an additional five thousand - in exchange for her three ships, all trading goods on board, and the strongest of her [dragons](wiki:Dragons): [Drogon](wiki:Drogon).(wiki:Targaryen_campaign_in_Slaver's_Bay.cite_ref-Rasos27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

## Origins

Following a suggestion from Ser [Jorah Mormont](wiki:Jorah_Mormont),(wiki:Fall_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Rasos8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) [Daenerys Targaryen](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) sails from [Qarth](wiki:Qarth) to [Astapor](wiki:Astapor) on *[Balerion](wiki:Balerion_(ship))* to purchase [Unsullied](wiki:Unsullied). There, Daenerys negotiates with [Kraznys mo Nakloz](wiki:Kraznys_mo_Nakloz) through the translator [Missandei](wiki:Missandei). Daenerys understands the [High Valyrian](wiki:High_Valyrian) of Astapor, but keeps Kraznys ignorant of this fact, as he continually insults her. Daenerys is impressed with the martial skill of the Unsullied, but is horrified by their mistreatment.(wiki:Fall_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Rasos23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) She agreed to purchase all of the Unsullied soldiers in exchange for her ships, all of the trade goods she possessed, and [Drogon](wiki:Drogon), her largest dragon.(wiki:Fall_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Rasos27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

Other than the Unsullied, Astapor is poorly defended; its walls are old and crumbling, with no guards in the towers. Even a modest [Dothraki](wiki:Dothraki) *[khalasar](wiki:Khalasar)* could probably take the city, but none has ever tried because of the Unsullied.(wiki:Fall_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Rasos23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1) Apparently, no one thought it was a bad idea to turn control of all of their best soldiers to an ambitious buyer such as the desperate queen inside their walls.

## Narrative Arc

The exchange takes place the next morning, at the [Plaza of Punishment](wiki:Plaza_of_Punishment). Having closed the deal, Daenerys receives the [harpy's fingers](wiki:Harpy's_fingers), a nine-lashed whip that symbolizes her ownership of the Unsullied. She betrayed the terms of the deal and unleashes her dragons on the masters and the few remaining soldiers in the plaza, ordering the Unsullied to kill the remaining slave-masters of the city and freeing all of the slaves.(wiki:Fall_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Rasos27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2)

## Aftermath

Before leaving for [Yunkai](wiki:Yunkai), Daenerys leaves a council of three men—a healer, a scholar and a priest—to rule. They are later deposed by [Cleon](wiki:Cleon), a former butcher, who leads the people to believe that the three intend to return the [Good Masters](wiki:Good_Masters) to their positions of power. Naming himself king after having the council executed, Cleon has all the highborn boys seized to begin training new Unsullied. Each pyramid within Astapor is now an armed camp and the dead lie in the streets and the markets of devoid of food and slaves to purchase.(wiki:Fall_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Rasos71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) The city is later [besieged by the Yunkai'i](wiki:Siege_of_Astapor).(wiki:Fall_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

Davos thinks Stannis means another battle against the [Lannisters](wiki:House_Lannister), but [Melisandre](wiki:Melisandre) explains to Davos that it is the great battle Stannis is speaking of. Stannis then tells Davos that he saw the great battle in the flames. Davos is astonished, as it is not like Stannis to lie about such a thing. Stannis tells him that after the [Battle of the Blackwater](wiki:Battle_of_the_Blackwater) he was lost in despair but Melisandre bid him to gaze into the hearth fire; he describes to Davos what he saw in the flames,

> The ashes were white, rising in the updraft, yet all at once it seemed as if they were falling. Snow, I thought. Then the sparks in the air seemed to circle, to become a ring of torches, and I was looking **through** the fire down on some [high hill in a forest](wiki:Fist_of_the_First_Men). For all the heat of the fire, [I felt a cold so terrible](wiki:Others) I shivered, and when I did the sight was gone, the fire but a fire once again. After the [battle beneath the Wall](wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall) Melisandre states that the war they have come to fight is a war for life itself. Melisandre tells Maester [Aemon](wiki:Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I)) that [the prince that was promised](wiki:The_prince_that_was_promised) is standing before him, King Stannis Baratheon, who is [Azor Ahai](wiki:Azor_Ahai) reborn and that in him the prophecies are fulfilled.(wiki:War_for_the_dawn.cite_ref-Rasos78.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

The abduction of Lyanna Stark by Prince Rhaegar Targaryen near Harrenhal at the start of 282 AC — the inciting act of Robert's Rebellion. Following Rhaegar's crowning of Lyanna as queen of love and beauty at the [Tourney at Harrenhal](wiki:Tourney_at_Harrenhal) ("the moment when all smiles died"), Rhaegar took Lyanna, betrothed of Robert Baratheon. Her brother Brandon Stark rode to King's Landing to demand her return, setting the chain of events that became the rebellion in motion.

Meanwhile, he starts raising a host at [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun) and sends [Lord Vance](wiki:Lord_Vance) of [Wayfarer's Rest](wiki:Wayfarer's_Rest) and Lord [Clement Piper](wiki:Clement_Piper) of [Pinkmaiden](wiki:Pinkmaiden) to guard the pass below the [Golden Tooth](wiki:Golden_Tooth), to prevent any Lannister armies from entering the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands).(wiki:Battle_in_the_hills_below_the_Golden_Tooth.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)

Tywin dispatches Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) to raid the villages along the [Red Fork](wiki:Red_Fork), and Gregor circumvents the [Tully](wiki:House_Tully) forces below the Golden Tooth. In response Edmure further divides his forces, sending men to every village and holdfast within a day's ride of the border. Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), fears this will weaken the defense of the riverlands.(wiki:Battle_in_the_hills_below_the_Golden_Tooth.cite_ref-Ragot43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

## Narrative Arc

Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister), commanding the Lannister forces, descends on the outnumbered [rivermen](wiki:Rivermen) and breaks them. Lord Vance is slain during the fight and Lord Piper retreats back to [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun) with the remnants of his forces, with Jaime following on his heels. Meanwhile, Tywin brings another Lannister army up from the south.(wiki:Battle_in_the_hills_below_the_Golden_Tooth.cite_ref-Ragot55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

## Aftermath

The rout of the rivermen beneath the Golden Tooth allows Jaime to then fight the [battle under the walls of Riverrun](wiki:Battle_under_the_walls_of_Riverrun).(wiki:Battle_in_the_hills_below_the_Golden_Tooth.cite_ref-Ragot59.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) Ser [Marq Piper](wiki:Marq_Piper) and [Karyl Vance](wiki:Karyl_Vance), now a lord, raid behind the Lannisters' lines.(wiki:Battle_in_the_hills_below_the_Golden_Tooth.cite_ref-Ragot56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) Jaime later thinks of the battle as a glorious victory.(wiki:Battle_in_the_hills_below_the_Golden_Tooth.cite_ref-Raffc8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

## Origins

The riots are caused by lack of [food](wiki:Food) and a sense of dissatisfaction among the general populace during the preparation of [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), acting [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), for the eventual assault on King's Landing by [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), a claimant to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne). With Lord [Mace Tyrell](wiki:Mace_Tyrell) cutting off supplies from the [Reach](wiki:Reach) coming up the [roseroad](wiki:Roseroad), and the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) awash in blood, King's Landing is supplied mainly by [House Rosby](wiki:House_Rosby) and [House Stokeworth](wiki:House_Stokeworth), whose lands have been largely untouched by the war. This leads to shortages in most basic foods, and most of the incoming provisions are allocated to the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) and the garrison. A baker is killed in his oven for allegedly charging too much for his bread.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)

## Aftermath

The [small council](wiki:Small_council) extends the curfew, bringing more peace to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing).(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1) Food remains scarce, however, and the price of new seafood soars.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) Ser [Jacelyn Bywater](wiki:Jacelyn_Bywater) is unsure of the discipline and loyalty of the [gold cloaks](wiki:Gold_cloaks) under his command, and he reveals to [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister) that the [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk) mostly blame Tyrion, the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), for the city's troubles instead of young King [Joffrey I Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon).(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-10) Tyrion has a [new High Septon](wiki:High_Septon_(Tyrion's)) named, and Ser [Balon Swann](wiki:Balon_Swann) fills Ser [Preston Greenfield](wiki:Preston_Greenfield)'s place in the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard).(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-1)

[Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark) has nightmares of the riot.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok52.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) Queen Regent [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) plans for Prince [Tommen Baratheon](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon) to be discretely moved to [Rosby](wiki:Rosby) for his own safety.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2) [Tyrek Lannister](wiki:Tyrek_Lannister) remains missing, despite the efforts of Jacelyn, Lord [Varys](wiki:Varys), and Ser [Addam Marbrand](wiki:Addam_Marbrand) to find him.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Rasos4.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) notes that Varys was not present to see Princess [Myrcella Baratheon](wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon) off to Dorne, thereby avoiding the riot, and he wonders if Varys had agents abduct Tyrek because the youth had squired for King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon).(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Raffc27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)

While Lord [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish) meets with the [Tyrells](wiki:House_Tyrell) in [Highgarden](wiki:Highgarden), Petyr's men spread tales of the mob killing Preston and raping [Lollys Stokeworth](wiki:Lollys_Stokeworth), a way to discretely encourage Ser [Loras Tyrell](wiki:Loras_Tyrell) to join the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) and thereby defend his sister, [Margaery Tyrell](wiki:Margaery_Tyrell), who is betrothed to Joffrey.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) Lollys eventually has a son who is named [Tyrion Tanner](wiki:Tyrion_Tanner).(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Radwdappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1)

After becoming Hand, Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) replaces the stolen and smashed crystal [crown](wiki:Crown) of the High Septon with an even grander one of crystal and spun [gold](wiki:Gold).(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Rasos60.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)

During Tyrion's trial for [Joffrey's death](wiki:Purple_Wedding), Balon verifies that Tyrion had kicked Joffrey on the day of the riot.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Racok66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)

The night before her [walk of atonement](wiki:Walk_of_atonement) through the streets of the capital, a fearful Cersei recalls the bread riots.(wiki:Riots_in_King's_Landing.cite_ref-Radwd65.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

## Identity

At the war council in ADWD The Lost Lord, immediately after [Jon Connington](jon-connington) reveals the hidden prince ([Aegon is revealed to the Golden Company](aegon-revealed-to-the-golden-company)), [Aegon Targaryen](aegon-targaryen-young-griff) — goaded by [Tyrion Lannister](tyrion-lannister)'s challenge to prove himself rather than wait on [Daenerys Targaryen](daenerys-targaryen) — declares he will "sail west instead of east." He declines the option to march east to join his aunt in Meereen and sets aside the Volantene/Yunkai sellsword-contract path, choosing instead to claim the Iron Throne by himself. Over the caution of captain-general [Harry Strickland](harry-strickland), the captains of the Golden Company rise one by one, kneel, and lay their swords at his feet. This decision is the pivot of the entire invasion: the crossing to Cape Wrath in the stormlands follows directly from it.

The broken/declined Volantis–Yunkai contract is folded into this beat as a qualifier (a non-decision — "I would think on it"), not a separate node.

## Origins

King [Aegon IV Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen)'s trueborn son and heir, [Daeron](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen), was born to his sister-wife [Naerys](wiki:Naerys_Targaryen) in [153 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest). Seventeen years later, Aegon's cousin, Princess [Daena Targaryen](wiki:Daena_Targaryen), gave birth to the king's eldest known [bastard](wiki:Bastard) son, [Daemon Waters](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre).(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_baelor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) King Aegon IV publicly acknowledged Daemon in [182 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest) and gave him the [Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen) [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel) sword [Blackfyre](wiki:Blackfyre),(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1)(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-greatbastards_8-0) which had previously been passed on from king to king ever since King [Aegon I Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_I_Targaryen), the Conqueror. Because of this, there were some who felt that the sword symbolized the monarchy,(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-9) and that Aegon's public gift of the sword was an indication that he wanted Daemon to succeed him. Aegon's gift would eventually start the first talks that Daemon should be king.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-targaryenhistory_10-0) Following the acquisition of the sword, Daemon took the name "Blackfyre" for himself.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2) On his deathbed in [184 AC](wiki:184_AC), King Aegon IV legitimized all of his bastard children, including Daemon.

In [196 AC](wiki:196_AC), Daemon claimed the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne), thereby challenging his half-brother, King [Daeron II Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen).(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1) The war that followed was known as the Blackfyre Rebellion (and decades later as the [First Blackfyre Rebellion](wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion)). It lasted nigh on a year, during which time battles were fought between the rebels and loyalists in the [Vale](wiki:Vale), the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands), the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) and the [Reach](wiki:Reach), among other places.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2)

The war came to an end after nearly a year at the [Redgrass Field](wiki:Redgrass_Field). On the eve of the battle, Ser [Quentyn Ball](wiki:Quentyn_Ball), one of Daemon's key generals, was slain by a common archer.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1) Meanwhile, Lord [Bracken](wiki:House_Bracken) had gone across the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea) to hire forces, but storms delayed the return of him and the [Myrish](wiki:Myr) crossbowmen he had hired.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

## Aftermath

Ten thousand men died on what would later be known as the [Redgrass Field](wiki:Redgrass_Field), presumably for all the blood sucked red.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-6) [Daemon Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre) and both of his eldest twin sons, [Aegon](wiki:Aegon_Blackfyre) and [Aemon](wiki:Aemon_Blackfyre), were slain in the battle, effectively ending the rebellion and confirming King [Daeron II's](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen) rule of the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms). However, [Bittersteel](wiki:Aegor_Rivers) fled to the [Free Cities](wiki:Free_Cities) with many others (including the five surviving sons and several daughters of Daemon)(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-4) and the [Blackfyre Pretenders](wiki:Blackfyre_Pretenders) continued to plague the [Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen) dynasty until [Maelys the Monstrous](wiki:Maelys_Blackfyre) was slain in the [Stepstones](wiki:Stepstones).

It is suggested that the Redgrass Field was a close thing and if Daemon had not stopped for Gwayne, he might have broken Maekar's left before Bloodraven had gained the ridge and Baelor arrived.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-7) Then, with the Hand slain and the road to King's Landing open, Daemon would have had little opposition.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-8)

Amongst the dead were Lady [Rohanne Webber](wiki:Rohanne_Webber)'s first husband, squire to Lord [Wyman Webber](wiki:Wyman_Webber);(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-9) [Roger of Pennytree](wiki:Roger_of_Pennytree), Ser [Arlan of Pennytree](wiki:Arlan_of_Pennytree)'s squire;(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-10)(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-5) and [Lord Hayford](wiki:Lord_Hayford)(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-11) slain by Lord [Gormon Peake](wiki:Gormon_Peake).(wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-6)

## Origins

[Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy) is sent to [Pyke](wiki:Pyke) as an envoy of [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark), [King in the North](wiki:King_in_the_North), offering his father [Balon Greyjoy](wiki:Balon_Greyjoy), [Lord of the Iron Islands](wiki:Lord_of_the_Iron_Islands), an alliance and a strike against the [Lannisters](wiki:House_Lannister). Balon rejects the offer, however, and instead plans to strike at the vulnerable north.(wiki:Harrying_of_the_Stony_Shore.cite_ref-Racok11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) As part of the plan, Theon, accompanied by [Aeron Greyjoy](wiki:Aeron_Greyjoy) and [Dagmer Cleftjaw](wiki:Dagmer), is to take eight longships and harry the [Stony Shore](wiki:Stony_Shore), sinking any ship chance met.(wiki:Harrying_of_the_Stony_Shore.cite_ref-Racok24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-2)

Theon and his party attack a fishing village in the dark of the early morning, slaying most of the men and taking the women for [salt wives](wiki:Salt_wives) or [thralls](wiki:Thrall). A few men are allowed to escape and inform [Torrhen's Square](wiki:Torrhen's_Square).(wiki:Harrying_of_the_Stony_Shore.cite_ref-Racok37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

## Aftermath

After defeating the Wild Hares, Theon gives six of his eight longships to Aeron so he can continue harrying the coast, while the crew of Theon's *[Sea Bitch](wiki:Sea_Bitch)* and [Dagmer](wiki:Dagmer)'s *[Foamdrinker](wiki:Foamdrinker)* advance inland.(wiki:Harrying_of_the_Stony_Shore.cite_ref-Racok37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)(wiki:Harrying_of_the_Stony_Shore.cite_ref-Racok46.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0)

By killing Benfred's party, the true size of the assault by the ironmen is kept secret a little longer, which allows the [taking of Deepwood Motte](wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte) and an [attack on Torrhen's Square](wiki:Fight_at_Torrhen's_Square).
